item.title: Lloyds Plans SRT Tied to $4.2 Billion of Loans to Smaller Firms
item.description: Lloyds Banking Group Plc is looking to sell its first significant risk transfer deal of the year, linked to loans to small and medium-sized companies, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Why Doesn’t China Have a Lionel Messi?
item.description: Bloomberg Economics examines why the ‘China Model’ hasn’t created success on the football pitch.

item.title: ShopMy Turns Everyone Into an Influencer Seeking a Commission
item.description: The site raises questions about the motives behind product recommendations

item.title: Turkey Extends Rates Pause Despite Grim Inflation Outlook
item.description: Turkey’s central bank kept its main interest rate unchanged for a third consecutive time since the outbreak of the Iran war on the back of a cooling economy and subdued demand for hard currency.

item.title: World Cup Kicks Off Logistics Scramble Across North American Continent
item.description: A 5,000-vehicles fleet will shuttle a million pounds of team equipment to 104 matches.

item.title: ECB Set to Raise Interest Rates in First Hike Since 2023
South African Business Body Questions Rate Hike as More Seen
item.description: South Africa’s main industry body questioned the central bank’s decision to raise interest rates at a time of weak economic growth and fragile business sentiment amid the war in Iran.

item.title: ‘We’re Traders, Not Nuns’: Embattled Brazil Hedge-Fund Plots Turnaround
item.description: Rogerio Xavier wants to set some things straight.

item.title: Dangote Refinery Seeks to Raise $1 Billion in Private Debt Sale
item.description: Nigerian tycoon Aliko Dangote’s refinery unit is seeking to raise as much as $1 billion through a private sale of debt, people with knowledge of the plan said.

item.title: Heart Health Biotech Kardigan Seeks $373.3 Million in US IPO
item.description: Kardigan Inc. is seeking to raise $373.3 million in a US initial public offering to help fund three late-stage drugs that target cardiovascular diseases.

item.title: Takaichi Says 100% of Japan’s Oil Supply to Avoid Hormuz in July
item.description: Japan’s crude oil imports in July are expected to be fully sourced from areas that don’t require transit through the Strait of Hormuz, Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi said Thursday, as she seeks to diversify the resource-poor nation’s energy supply ahead of the hot summer months.

item.title: The Cost of AI Is Showing Up in Inflation
item.description: The massive buildout of data centers is driving up costs for critical hardware used in phones, computers and cars.

item.title: Wall Street’s Worries Are All About AI
item.description: Across bonds, credit and stocks, big names are putting their worries on record.

item.title: Grindr CEO Wants His Dating App to Become a Gay Culture Hub
item.description: Madonna made a surprise appearance in Times Square last week, promoting her latest album, Confessions II. It was a marketing win for the pop star — and an even bigger one for Grindr Inc.

item.title: CVC’s Lucas Says AI Impact on PE Portfolios Can’t Be Overstated
item.description: The importance of artificial intelligence for private equity portfolios can’t be overemphasized as managers look to prepare businesses they own for rampant technological change, according to CVC Capital Partners Plc.

item.title: Kretinsky Is Said to Consider Becoming Top West Ham Shareholder
item.description: Billionaire Daniel Kretinsky is considering a deal to become the largest shareholder in the London football team West Ham United, according to people familiar with the situation.

item.title: AI Boom Stokes Inflation With Memory Chips at ‘Insane’ Prices
item.description: When Chris Barber, who’s been in the computer business for a quarter-century, says he’s never seen anything like the AI boom, he’s not talking about its potential to transform the future. He means what it’s doing to hardware prices right now.

item.title: SpaceX IPO Will Mint Billions for a New Silicon Valley Hierarchy
item.description: The initial public offering for SpaceX is poised to generate billions of dollars in profits for the fortunate few investors who got in early on Elon Musk’s rocket, satellite and artificial intelligence company.

item.title: Warren Wants Wall Street to Disclose Data on AI Firm Exposure
item.description: Senator Elizabeth Warren introduced a bill requiring financial institutions to disclose their exposure to artificial intelligence companies, continuing her push for more scrutiny on the sector’s funding.

item.title: Bangladesh Targets Growth Revival With Record Budget Spending
item.description: Bangladesh’s new government unveiled its first budget on Thursday, outlining record spending to be financed through higher tax revenue and foreign borrowing, as the South Asian nation seeks to revive growth after a period of political turmoil and economic disruption.

item.title: Greek LNG Buyer Says Long-Term US Deals Getting More Difficult
item.description: The boss of Greece’s Atlantic SEE LNG Trade said it’s becoming more difficult to secure long-term liquefied natural gas deals with US suppliers, after the Iran war upended the global market.

item.title: Emerging-Market Stocks Drop as Chinese E-Commerce Joins AI Slide
item.description: Emerging-market stocks dropped for the sixth time in seven days as a regulatory censure sparked a selloff in Chinese e-commerce stocks and another flare-up in the Middle Eastern conflict sapped risk appetite.

item.title: Thai Hospitality Group Minor International Said to Mull Singapore IPO Instead of HK
item.description: Thailand’s Minor International Pcl is considering listing its restaurant business in Singapore instead of Hong Kong as it sees greater interest from potential investors in the city-state, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: US-Backed Philippines AI Hub Could Draw at Least $10 Billion in Investments
item.description: A US-backed plan to build an artificial intelligence hub in the Philippines could draw an initial investment of about $10 billion, an official said, and help prop up an economy battered by the Iran war.

item.title: Saudi IPO Delay Is Another Setback for the Lagging Stock Market
item.description: A postponed initial public offering in Saudi Arabia is the latest setback for a stock market that’s trailing global peers for the fourth year in a row.

item.title: SK Hynix ETF Boom Is Becoming a Force in the Stock’s Trading
item.description: One of the hottest artificial-intelligence stocks in South Korea is increasingly being driven by demand for products built on top of it.

item.title: South Africa Posts Largest Current-Account Surplus Since 2022
item.description: South Africa posted its largest current-account surplus in four years in the first quarter as the value of gold exports surged and imports dropped.

item.title: Why Indonesia’s Rupiah Keeps Hitting Record Lows
item.description: Indonesia’s rupiah — Asia’s worst-performing currency this year — has been under pressure since late 2024 when investors began reassessing the country’s fiscal outlook following the election of President Prabowo Subianto. Before this current period of volatility, the currency hadn’t traded at such weak levels since the Asian Financial Crisis of 1997-98.

item.title: How Houthis’ Red Sea Shipping Threat Risks Bigger Oil Shock
item.description: One thing that’s helped limit the Iran war’s disruption to global oil supply is the ability of Saudi Arabia to use an alternative export route to the Strait of Hormuz: the Red Sea.

item.title: Blackstone to Back Two Sigma Veteran’s Quant Hedge Fund Startup
item.description: Jinger Zhao, a former Two Sigma Investments managing director who spent 18 years at the asset manager, is preparing to start her own Asia-focused hedge fund with initial backing from Blackstone Inc.
